1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()
86

1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None
66

2	from typing import List
3	
4	from .models import Bookmark
5	
6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/src/models.py`

The `Bookmark` dataclass has the following fields:
- **id** (str): Unique identifier (UUID string)
- **url** (str): The URL being bookmarked
- **title** (str): Display name for the bookmark
- **tags** (List[str]): List of tag strings (default: empty list)
- **notes** (str): Additional notes/description (default: empty string)
- **created_at** (str): ISO format timestamp (auto-set to current time if not provided)

Key methods:
- `to_dict()` - Converts bookmark to dictionary for JSON serialization
- `from_dict(cls, data)` - Class method to reconstruct from dictionary

---

## 2. CLI Structure

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/src/cli.py`

**Current Commands:**

1. **add** - Add a new bookmark
   - Required: `url`, `title`
   - Optional: `--tags` (list), `--notes` (string)

2. **list** - List all bookmarks
   - Optional: `--format` (choices: "table", "json"; default: "table")

3. **delete** - Delete a bookmark by ID
   - Required: `id`

4. **show** - Display a single bookmark
   - Required: `id`

**CLI Architecture:**
- `build_parser()` - Creates the argument parser with subcommands
- `main(argv=None)` - Entry point that handles command dispatch
- Uses argparse for command-line parsing
- Entry point defined in `pyproject.toml` as `bookmark = "src.cli:main"`

---

## 3. Storage Layer

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/src/storage.py`

**Persistence:**
- JSON file stored at `~/.bookmarks.json`
- Bookmarks are stored as an array of objects

**Key Functions:**
- `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - Loads all bookmarks from JSON file (returns empty list if missing)
- `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` - Writes bookmarks to JSON file with 2-space indentation
- `add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path)` - Creates and persists a new bookmark with UUID
- `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Removes bookmark by ID, returns bool success
- `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Retrieves single bookmark by ID, returns None if not found

**Design Pattern:** Load-modify-save pattern for all operations

---

## 4. Formatter (Output)

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/src/formatter.py`

**Functions:**
- `format_table(bookmarks)` - Formats as plain-text table with columns:
  - ID (8 chars)
  - Title (30 chars)
  - URL (40 chars)
  - Tags (20 chars)
  - Shows only first 8 chars of UUID
  - Returns "No bookmarks found." if empty

- `format_json(bookmarks)` - Outputs full bookmark objects as JSON with 2-space indentation

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/tests/test_storage.py` - 13 tests for storage operations
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/tests/test_search.py` - Search functionality test definitions

- Using `assert result == []`, `assert len(result) == N`
- Using `assert result.field == value`
- Checking file existence with `os.path.exists()`
- Validating JSON with `json.load()`

Defines expected search functionality (not yet implemented). Tests import from non-existent `src.search` module:

**Expected Functions:**
- `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` - Case-insensitive substring match in title
- `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` - Exact tag matching
- `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` - Match domain/subdomain from URL
- `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)` - Combined search with AND logic

**Test Coverage:**
- Case-insensitive matching
- Substring/substring-in-list matching
- Empty result handling

- No criteria returns all bookmarks

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775313178185-lyxl2r/pyproject.toml`

```toml
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project.scripts]
bookmark = "src.cli:main"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
```

## 7. Key Implementation Details for Feature Planning

**URL/Domain Parsing Requirements:**
Based on test_search.py, the search functionality needs:
- Extract domain from URLs like `https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html` → match against `docs.python.org`
- Support subdomain matching (e.g., `docs.python.org` matches both `docs.python.org` URLs and would need to check if it matches `python.org`)
- Test cases show:
  - `github.com` should match 2 bookmarks (github.com/runpod/runpod-python and github.com/pallets/flask)
  - `docs.python.org` should match 2 bookmarks (two different paths on that domain)

2. Handler calls storage functions (load/add/delete/get)
3. Storage layer reads/writes JSON file
4. Results formatted via formatter functions
5. Output printed to stdout

**Error Handling:**
- Missing files return empty list or None
- Non-existent IDs return None or False
- CLI exits with code 1 on errors (missing bookmark)

---

## Summary

This is a straightforward bookmark management CLI with JSON-based persistence. The architecture is clean and modular:
- **Models** define the data structure
- **Storage** handles persistence
- **CLI** manages user interaction
- **Formatter** handles output presentation
- **Tests** use pytest with fixtures and temporary files

The codebase is ready for the **search feature** implementation, which requires:
1. New `src/search.py` module with 4 functions
2. URL domain extraction logic
3. CLI integration in `cli.py` with new "search" subcommand

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter or search bookmarks. Users need to find bookmarks by title keyword, tag, or domain. The test file `tests/test_search.py` already exists and fully specifies the expected behavior — the goal is to implement code that makes all those tests pass without breaking existing tests.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **Create new** — all search logic lives here |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** — add `search` subcommand to `build_parser()` and a handler branch in `main()` |
| `src/models.py` | Read-only reference — `Bookmark` dataclass with fields: `id`, `url`, `title`, `tags` (List[str]), `notes`, `created_at` |
| `src/formatter.py` | Reuse — `format_table(bookmarks)` and `format_json(bookmarks)` already accept `List[Bookmark]` |
| `src/storage.py` | Reuse — `load_bookmarks()` already loads all bookmarks |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Read-only — defines every assertion the implementation must satisfy |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against any tag in the bookmark's tag list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL netloc equals the given domain string."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combine all criteria with AND logic. No criteria returns all bookmarks."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Key decisions:**
- `search_by_domain` uses `urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc` — stdlib, no new dependencies.  
  - `https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python` → netloc `github.com` ✓  
  - `https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html` → netloc `docs.python.org` ✓  
- `search` chains calls so AND logic is implicit (each filter narrows the previous result).
- When no criteria are provided, `search` returns the original list unchanged (satisfies `test_no_criteria_returns_all`).

---

### Step 2 — Add `search` subcommand to `src/cli.py`

**In `build_parser()`**, append after the `show` parser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

**In `main()`**, add a new `elif` branch after the `show` block:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    from .search import search
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

**Reused functions:** `load_bookmarks()` (already imported), `format_table()` and `format_json()` (already imported).

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite from the project root:

```bash
pytest
```

Expected outcome:
- All pre-existing tests in `tests/test_storage.py` continue to pass (no changes to storage or models).
- All tests in `tests/test_search.py` pass:
  - `TestSearchByTitle` (3 tests) — case-insensitive, substring, no-match
  - `TestSearchByTag` (3 tests) — exact tag, shared tag, no-match
  - `TestSearchByDomain` (3 tests) — domain, subdomain-as-input, no-match
  - `TestSearchCombined` (7 tests) — single criteria, AND logic, all-three, no-criteria, conflicting
  - `TestSearchCLI` (2 tests) — parser accepts `search` + `--title`/`--tag`/`--domain`/`--format`

# Plan: Add Search Functionality to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI (`src/cli.py`) currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter or search bookmarks. Users need to find bookmarks by title keyword, tag, or domain. The test file `tests/test_search.py` already exists and fully specifies the expected behavior — the goal is to implement code that makes all those tests pass without breaking existing tests.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **Create new** — all search logic lives here |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** — add `search` subcommand to `build_parser()` and a handler branch in `main()` |
| `src/models.py` | Read-only reference — `Bookmark` dataclass with fields: `id`, `url`, `title`, `tags` (List[str]), `notes`, `created_at` |
| `src/formatter.py` | Reuse — `format_table(bookmarks)` and `format_json(bookmarks)` already accept `List[Bookmark]` |
| `src/storage.py` | Reuse — `load_bookmarks()` already loads all bookmarks |
| `tests/test_search.py` | Read-only — defines every assertion the implementation must satisfy |

---

## Step-by-Step Implementation

### Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on bookmark title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against any tag in the bookmark's tag list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL netloc equals the given domain string."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combine all criteria with AND logic. No criteria returns all bookmarks."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Key decisions:**
- `search_by_domain` uses `urllib.parse.urlparse(url).netloc` — stdlib, no new dependencies.  
  - `https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python` → netloc `github.com` ✓  
  - `https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html` → netloc `docs.python.org` ✓  
- `search` chains calls so AND logic is implicit (each filter narrows the previous result).
- When no criteria are provided, `search` returns the original list unchanged (satisfies `test_no_criteria_returns_all`).

---

### Step 2 — Add `search` subcommand to `src/cli.py`

**In `build_parser()`**, append after the `show` parser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Search by title (substring)")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Search by tag (exact)")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Search by URL domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

**In `main()`**, add a new `elif` branch after the `show` block:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    from .search import search
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

**Reused functions:** `load_bookmarks()` (already imported), `format_table()` and `format_json()` (already imported).

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite from the project root:

```bash
pytest
```

Expected outcome:
- All pre-existing tests in `tests/test_storage.py` continue to pass (no changes to storage or models).
- All tests in `tests/test_search.py` pass:
  - `TestSearchByTitle` (3 tests) — case-insensitive, substring, no-match
  - `TestSearchByTag` (3 tests) — exact tag, shared tag, no-match
  - `TestSearchByDomain` (3 tests) — domain, subdomain-as-input, no-match
  - `TestSearchCombined` (7 tests) — single criteria, AND logic, all-three, no-criteria, conflicting
  - `TestSearchCLI` (2 tests) — parser accepts `search` + `--title`/`--tag`/`--domain`/`--format`

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Tests for search functionality.

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs
to be implemented in src/search.py and wired into the CLI.
"""

import json
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title


@pytest.fixture
def bookmarks():
    return [
        Bookmark(
            id="aaa-111",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html",
            title="Python Tutorial",
            tags=["python", "tutorial", "docs"],
            notes="Official Python tutorial",
            created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="bbb-222",
            url="https://github.com/runpod/runpod-python",
            title="RunPod Python SDK",
            tags=["python", "sdk", "runpod"],
            notes="RunPod serverless SDK",
            created_at="2026-01-02T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ccc-333",
            url="https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript",
            title="MDN JavaScript Guide",
            tags=["javascript", "docs", "mdn"],
            notes="Comprehensive JS reference",
            created_at="2026-01-03T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="ddd-444",
            url="https://github.com/pallets/flask",
            title="Flask Web Framework",
            tags=["python", "flask", "web"],
            notes="Lightweight WSGI web framework",
            created_at="2026-01-04T00:00:00",
        ),
        Bookmark(
            id="eee-555",
            url="https://docs.python.org/3/library/argparse.html",
            title="argparse documentation",
            tags=["python", "cli", "docs"],
            notes="Argparse module reference",
            created_at="2026-01-05T00:00:00",
        ),
    ]


# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_single_criterion_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

    def test_single_criterion_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="docs", domain="docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--title", "python"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.title == "python"

    def test_search_format_json_flag(self):
        """The CLI should accept --format json on the search command."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact tag match."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL domain matches exactly."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Search bookmarks by combining criteria with AND logic.

    Returns all bookmarks when no criteria are given.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")

        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )

    # --- list ---

    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],

    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")

            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,

            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
